content: Phase I trials are the first stage of human testing for investigational drugs, primarily focused on safety and tolerability. These studies typically enroll 20-100 healthy volunteers (except in oncology where patients are used). The primary objective is to determine the maximum tolerated dose (MTD), characterize pharmacokinetics (absorption, distribution, metabolism, excretion), and identify dose-limiting toxicities. Phase I trials usually last several months and establish the safety profile before proceeding to efficacy testing.
